assistant.title: Assistant
assistant.subtitle: Read-only Claude beta
assistant.deepModeHint: Use the more capable Sonnet model for this reply. Costs more — daily limit applies.
assistant.inputPlaceholder: Ask about your projects, tasks, or ideas…

welcome.title: Hi! I'm here to help.
welcome.body: I can read your projects and tasks to summarize, suggest priorities, and find things. I can't edit anything yet — that's coming soon.
welcome.bodyReadWrite: I can read and organize your work — create and update tasks, set priorities, draft routines, and tidy up projects. Just tell me what you need.

quickActions.lastWeek: What did I work on last week?

projects.title: You'll be over your projects quota
projects.body: You have {current} active projects. When you drop to Free on {date}, the plan allows only {cap}. Archive {over_by} before then to avoid being blocked.

tasks_total.title: You'll be over your tasks quota
tasks_total.body: You have {current} open tasks. When you drop to Free on {date}, the plan allows only {cap}. Close or delete {over_by} before then.

routines.title: You'll be over your routines quota
routines.body: You have {current} active routines. When you drop to Free on {date}, the plan allows only {cap}. Archive {over_by} before then.

ideas.title: You'll be over your ideas quota
ideas.body: You have {current} ideas. When you drop to Free on {date}, the plan allows only {cap}. Delete or promote {over_by} before then.

categories.title: You'll be over your categories quota
categories.body: You have {current} categories. When you drop to Free on {date}, the plan allows only {cap}. Delete {over_by} before then.

projects.title: Project quota exceeded
projects.body: You have {current} active projects but the {plan} plan allows only {cap}. Archive {over_by} to avoid getting blocked when creating new ones.

tasks_total.title: Task quota exceeded
tasks_total.body: You have {current} open tasks but the {plan} plan allows only {cap}. Close or delete {over_by} to get back under the limit.

routines.title: Routine quota exceeded
routines.body: You have {current} active routines but the {plan} plan allows only {cap}. Archive {over_by} to get back under the limit.

ideas.title: Ideas quota exceeded
ideas.body: You have {current} ideas but the {plan} plan allows only {cap}. Delete or promote {over_by} to projects.

categories.title: Categories quota exceeded
categories.body: You have {current} categories but the {plan} plan allows only {cap}. Delete {over_by} to get back under the limit.

quota.default: You hit your {plan} plan limit ({current}/{cap}). Upgrade to keep going.
quota.projects: You hit your active projects limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}). Archive one or upgrade.
quota.tasks_total: You hit your open tasks limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}). Complete some or upgrade.
quota.tasks_per_project: You hit the per-project task limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}).
quota.routines: You hit your active routines limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}). Archive one or upgrade.
quota.ideas: You hit your ideas limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}). Promote one to a project or delete it.
quota.categories: You hit your categories limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}).
quota.notes_per_project: You hit the per-project notes limit on the {plan} plan ({current}/{cap}).

header.streakTooltipActive: Current streak: {current, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} · Best: {best}
header.streakTooltipBest: Best streak: {best, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}. Log activity today to start a new run.
header.weekTooltip: Active on {count} of 7 days this week

step1.heading: Welcome, builder.
step1.sub: This takes 90 seconds. What should I call you?
step1.label: First name
step1.helperPrefilled: From your Google account. Edit it if you want.

step2.heading: How should this look?
step2.sub: You can change this anytime in Settings.

step4Beta.heading: Thanks for being here, {name}.
step4Beta.body: You're on the {plan} plan, no charge. You're part of the first builders testing this.
step4Beta.body2: If something breaks or feels off, tell me. Reply to any email I send. I read all of them.

step4Plan.heading: Pick a plan, or don't.
step4Plan.sub: Free works fine for most builders starting out. You can change this whenever.

replay.banner: Editing your setup. Saving applies to your account.

step1.title: Projects live here.
step1.body: Each one is active, paused with intention, or killed. None of them sit in limbo.

step2.title: Tasks belong to a project.
step2.body: If there's no project, there's no task. That's the rule.

step3.title: Routines are what you do without thinking.
step3.body: The stuff that happens every day. Track it here. The rest is noise.

step4.title: Stuck? Ask your AI.
step4.body: This button opens the assistant. It reads your projects and tasks, and answers in your context.

finalCta.title: Now build something.
finalCta.body: Create your first project. Even if you're not sure what it is yet.
finalCta.more: There's more (ideas, log, analytics, chat) but we don't want to bore you. Here are the links when you need them. Anything else, ask your AI.

appearance.title: Appearance & language
appearance.description: Pick how the app looks and the language you read it in.
appearance.languageHint: Affects the app interface and any digests/notifications you receive.
appearance.themeHint: Choose your preferred appearance. "System" follows your device setting.
appearance.paletteHint: Changes the accent colors only. Theme controls light/dark; palette swaps the highlights.

billing.title: Billing & plan
billing.description: Manage your subscription, payment method, and invoices.
billing.freeBlurb: Upgrade to Pro or Studio when you need more room or more AI.
billing.exemptBlurb: Your account has the {plan} plan free of charge, courtesy of the team. You won't see Stripe charges while this exemption is active.
billing.renewsAtWithDays: Next charge on {date} — in {days, plural, =0 {today} =1 {1 day} other {# days}}
billing.cancelScheduledBlurb: Your {plan} plan stays active until {date} ({days, plural, =0 {today} =1 {1 day remaining} other {# days remaining}}). After that you'll automatically move to Free. Your projects and data are kept.
billing.upgradeSectionTitlePro: Take your account to the next level
billing.manageOnWebHint: Plan changes, payment methods, and invoices are managed on the web.

billingPeriod.label: Billing period
billingPeriod.confirmBodyToAnnual: We'll switch your {plan} plan to annual billing. You'll go from {currentPrice} USD/month to {newPrice} USD/month billed once a year. Stripe will prorate the difference automatically.
billingPeriod.confirmBodyToMonthly: We'll switch your {plan} plan to monthly billing. You'll go from {currentPrice} USD/month (billed annually) to {newPrice} USD/month. The change applies from your next cycle.
billingPeriod.successAnnual: Done. You're now on annual billing.
billingPeriod.successMonthly: Done. You're now on monthly billing.

downgradeConfirm.title: Downgrade to Pro?
downgradeConfirm.body: You'll switch from Studio to Pro immediately. Stripe will prorate the difference on your next invoice — you'll see an automatic credit. All your projects, tasks, and conversations stay.
downgradeConfirm.noteAnnual: Your current plan is annual. The downgrade leaves you on Pro monthly; if you prefer Pro annual, change it from 'Manage subscription' afterwards.
downgradeConfirm.success: Done. Your plan is now Pro.

retention.title: Downgrade to Free
retention.stepOfferBodyTooExpensive: Stay with us and we'll apply 30% off for the next 3 months, automatically.
retention.stepOfferTitleNotUsed: Want us to help you use it more?
retention.stepOfferBodyNotUsed: We'll apply 25% off for the next 3 months and send weekly tips to get more out of it.
retention.stepOfferTitleMissingFeatures: We want to know what's missing.
retention.stepOfferBodyMissingFeatures: Our team reads your feedback. Meanwhile, here's 25% off for the next 3 months.
retention.stepOfferBodySwitching: We'll apply 20% off for the next 3 months in case you want to reconsider.
retention.stepOfferBodyTrialOnly: 30% off your next month so you can decide without pressure.
retention.stepOfferBodyOther: 20% off for the next 3 months as a thank you.
retention.stepDowngradeBody: Pro ({price}/mo) keeps the assistant with write mode, brainstorming, integrations and Deep Mode. Only the caps go down.
retention.stepConfirmBody: Your current plan stays active until {date}. After that, you'll be on the Free plan. Your projects, tasks, and ideas are kept — only the plan limits change.
retention.stepDoneTitleCancelled: Done. We'll move you to Free on {date}.
retention.stepDoneBodyCancelled: You keep full access until then. Change your mind anytime — you can resubscribe from here.
retention.stepDoneBodyOffer: You'll see the adjustment on your next invoice. Thanks for sticking around.
retention.stepDoneBodyDowngrade: Stripe prorated the change. You'll see the adjustment on your next invoice.
retention.retentionAlreadyUsed: You've already used your retention offer. Let's go straight to confirm the downgrade to Free.

billing.checkoutSuccess: All set! Your plan will activate as soon as Stripe confirms the payment.
billing.checkoutCancelled: You cancelled the payment. Try again whenever you're ready.
billing.checkoutError: Couldn't open checkout. Please try again in a moment.
billing.portalError: Couldn't open the billing portal. Please try again in a moment.

meta.title: continuu.it — Finish what you start
meta.description: The project manager for people who start more than they finish. Built for founders, freelancers, and side-project builders who keep abandoning what matters most.

hero.eyebrow: Currently in private beta. 50 spots available.
hero.headline: Finish what you start.
hero.subheadline: The project manager for people who start more than they finish. Built for founders, freelancers, and side-project builders who keep abandoning what matters most.
hero.ctaSecondary: No credit card. Lifetime free for the first 50 testers.

recognition.headline: You have a graveyard of unfinished projects. You're not alone. You're not lazy.
recognition.p1: Open your Notion. Count the half-written docs.
recognition.p2: Open your GitHub. Count the private repos.
recognition.p3: Open your bookmarks. Count the tools you bought once and never opened.
recognition.p4: You are not unmotivated. You are not unintelligent. You are not lacking discipline.
recognition.p5: You have been using productivity tools designed for the wrong problem.
recognition.callout: Five months on an ERP I never deployed. A web agency while my own websites went stale. A fantasy football tool while my team waited on me. Three weeks coding around a 30-minute bug.

diagnosis.headline: Every productivity tool was built to help you start. None were built to help you finish.
diagnosis.p1: Todoist makes it frictionless to add a task. Notion makes it elegant to start a new doc. Things makes it satisfying to plan a project.
diagnosis.p2: These tools all reward the dopamine hit of beginning.
diagnosis.p3: That's why you have 47 projects, 12 systems, and 3 productivity apps open right now, and still feel behind on everything.
diagnosis.p4: The problem isn't capture. It's closure.

promise.headline: The project manager that closes the loop.
promise.p1: continuu.it is the first tool designed around a single principle: every project you start must reach a decision.
promise.p3: Never abandoned. Never drifting. Never lost in the graveyard of 'I'll get to it later.'
promise.p4: Because the cost of unfinished work isn't time. It's competitive position. It's trust. It's the slow erosion of your relationship with your own ideas.
promise.p5: We built continuu.it to give you that back.

intro.headline: Six rituals that turn drift into shipped work.

forcedClosure.label: Forced closure
forcedClosure.headline: No project drifts. Ever.
forcedClosure.body: Every project lives in one of four states: Active, Paused, Killed, or Completed. After 14 days of silence, continuu.it asks you which one you mean. The drift is what kills builders. We don't let it happen.

demo.prompt: You haven't touched this in 14 days. Are you ready to ship? Need help? Or is this project done?

aiChat.label: AI chat
aiChat.headline: Tell the AI what you're building. It breaks it into tasks that actually move you forward.
aiChat.body: Not generic 'Plan, Build, Test, Launch' templates. Real, specific, actionable next steps tailored to your project, your stack, your scope. You just have to be honest about what you want to build.

demo.userMessage: I want to build a landing page for my SaaS in Next.js, but I always get stuck on what to do first.
questions: Do you already have copy written, or do you need to start with messaging?
Are you using a design system or starting from scratch?
demo.footer: Once I know these, I'll break this into 8–12 tasks ordered by what unblocks what.

aiChat.closing: Most projects don't die from lack of effort. They die from lack of structure. We fix that on day one.

resumeContext.label: AI resume context
resumeContext.headline: Come back to projects without losing where you were.
resumeContext.body: You paused a project three weeks ago. You're ready to pick it back up. But you've forgotten where you were, what you decided, and what was blocking you. continuu.it solves that.

demo.title: Welcome back to Landing Page Redesign
demo.lastWorked: You last worked on this 23 days ago.
demo.nextAction: Write hero copy for new pricing section
blockers: Design tokens not migrated to v2

resumeContext.closing: The cost of returning drops to zero. Projects you wrote off start finishing themselves.

notifications.label: Telegram + WhatsApp
notifications.headline: Wake up to your day. Get reminded before you lose it.
notifications.body: continuu.it meets you where you already are. Every morning, at the time you choose, it sends you a focused summary on Telegram. WhatsApp coming soon. Same workflow, your chosen channel.

demo.topPriorityNote: Active for 5 days, you committed to shipping it this week

notifications.closing: Notifications work best when they live where your attention already is.

friction.label: Friction where it matters
friction.headline: New ideas have friction. Existing commitments don't.
friction.body: Every time you add a new project in continuu.it, you see every active project you already have. Not to abandon them. Just to acknowledge them. To make a decision.

demo.newProjectPlaceholder: Workflow automation tool for indie hackers…
demo.modalTitle: Before you start a new one…
demo.modalBody: You already have 4 active projects. Which decision are you making?

friction.closing: Often, the decision is to ship one of them first.

sundayReview.label: Sunday review
sundayReview.headline: The most important hour of your week.
sundayReview.body: Every Sunday at a time you choose, continuu.it asks five questions. Ten minutes. One ritual. The mechanism that prevents next Friday from being a repeat of last Friday.

demo.title: Sunday review
demo.subtitle: Five questions. Ten minutes.
questions: What did you ship this week?
What blocker do you keep hitting?
What will you commit to next week?
What will you let go of?

sundayReview.closing: We didn't invent the weekly review. We just made it impossible to skip.

loopSociety.headline: Every builder is carrying a graveyard.
loopSociety.subheadline: The pattern repeats. The numbers don't lie.
loopSociety.body: This is the shape of the problem — the gravity continuu.it was built against.

one.label: Open projects
one.body: The number of half-finished projects the average builder carries in their head. Most never reach a decision.

two.label: Half-life
two.body: The point at which a side project drifts from "I'm working on it" to "I'll get back to it" — and never does.

three.label: Open right now
three.body: Productivity apps competing for your attention. None of them are designed to close a loop. All of them reward starting.

four.label: Until now
four.body: Built around forced closure. Every other tool makes starting easier. We make finishing the only way out.

howItWorks.headline: How continuu.it works

steps.title: Bring in what you're already building.
steps.body: Add your half-finished projects. The repo. The Notion doc. The side hustle. Then chat with the AI to break each one into tasks that make sense.

steps.title: Let the system meet you where you are.
steps.body: Get your morning briefing on Telegram. Set reminder times. Pick up paused projects with AI context. We close the loop on follow-up so you can stay in the flow of building.

steps.title: Show up on Sundays. Make decisions.
steps.body: continuu.it nudges, summarizes, reminds, and ritualizes. You just have to answer five questions every Sunday and pick what's next.

howItWorks.cta: Start your beta — free for life

beta.headline: 50 spots. Lifetime free Pro access.
beta.body: We're letting in the first 50 builders to test continuu.it before public launch. In exchange for your honest feedback, you get:
perks: Lifetime free access to Pro tier ($96/year value)
Direct line to the founder for feature requests
Influence on what we build next
Founding member badge in your profile
Early access to The Loop Society Premium avatars
beta.footer: Spots are filled on a first-come basis. We're looking for builders willing to articulate what they like and what they don't. No fluff. Just builders willing to help us build.

pricing.headline: Pick the plan that finishes with you.
pricing.subheadline: Start free. Upgrade when you need to.

free.name: Free
perks: Full Today view, analytics, and activity log

pro.name: Pro
perks: AI Assistant — 200 messages/day with write + brainstorming

studio.name: Studio
perks: WhatsApp — 500 messages/month + your assistant on WhatsApp (coming soon)
Outbound webhooks and personal API key
1:1 onboarding and early access to new features

pricing.betaBanner: Beta users keep Studio free, for life. No catch. Just feedback.

finalCall.headlineLine1: The graveyard of unfinished projects in your head is not your failure.
finalCall.headlineLine2: It's a structural problem with how productivity has been designed.
finalCall.p1: You have been given tools for starting. You have not been given tools for closing the loop.
finalCall.p2: Of course you have a graveyard. The system you were handed builds graveyards.
finalCall.p3: What if you stopped accepting that?
finalCall.p4: What if you decided, today, to do something about the seventeen things you started and never finished?
finalCall.p5: Even one of them. Pick one. Open it. Decide if it lives or dies. Make the call.
finalCall.p6: That's the whole game. The rest of your life is downstream of that single habit.
finalCall.subCta: Free for life. No credit card. 50 spots.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: How to stop running from the work that matters. Honest reflections on building, abandoning, and finishing.
